problem.description: Ever tried to create a visual on Canva? Or worse, configure software with 47 customization options? You have a business to run — not a design agency. The result: either you spend 2 hours cobbling something mediocre together, or you don't do it at all.

anatomy.title: Anatomy of a Stampeo card
anatomy.description: Every element is designed to be readable, professional, and compliant with Apple Wallet and Google Wallet constraints.

hero.title: One scan. One stamp. That's it.
hero.subtitle: No POS system. No special hardware. Your phone is all you need to stamp your customers in one second.

problem.title: Stamping should be invisible
problem.description: The customer pays, you look for the stamp, the card is at the bottom of the bag, the line grows. With a physical stamp, the act of rewarding loyalty slows down your service. And anything that slows service eventually disappears. Stamping should be as fast as contactless payment.

sections.title: Dedicated app
sections.description: The Stampeo app is lightweight and dedicated to scanning. Open the app, point the camera at the customer's QR code, and a stamp is added — with haptic confirmation. Total time: 1 second.

sections.title: Web scanner
sections.description: Don't want to install an app? Scan directly from your phone or tablet browser. Ideal for businesses using a shared tablet at the counter.

sections.title: Offline mode
sections.description: Works even without internet. Scans are saved locally and synced automatically when connection returns. No stamp is ever lost.

cta.title: Stamp in one second
cta.subtitle: Free app on iOS and Android. Web scanner available on all devices.

offline.title: Works even without Wi-Fi

employee.title: Built for your employees
employee.subtitle: Your employees aren't technicians. The interface is so simple that no training is needed.

features.title: Zero training
features.description: Open the app, scan, done. Your employees use it from day one.

features.title: Haptic feedback
features.description: Vibration and confirmation sound. Your employee knows the stamp was added without looking at the screen.

features.title: Scan history

team.title: Your whole team can scan
team.subtitle: Add your employees and manage access based on your plan.

security.title: Security and reliability
security.subtitle: Every scan is secure, traceable, and tamper-proof.

features.title: Unique QR codes
features.description: Each customer has a unique UUID encoded in their QR code. Impossible to duplicate or forge.

features.title: Server timestamps
features.description: Each stamp is server-timestamped with the employee ID and location.

features.title: Anti-fraud
features.description: Duplicate scan detection, rate limiting, and automatic alerts for suspicious activity.

features.title: End-to-end encryption
features.description: Scan data travels via HTTPS. No sensitive information is stored on the device.

hero.title: Every scan lands on your customer's lock screen.
hero.subtitle: Each stamp, milestone, and reward triggers a wallet notification. Your customer always sees what just happened and how close they are to the next reward.

problem.title: A stamp without a confirmation is a stamp forgotten
problem.description: With a paper card, the customer nods, pockets the card, and moves on. Nothing confirms the visit, nothing reminds them they're halfway to their reward. A wallet notification turns every visit into a silent follow-up: visible, instant, and impossible to miss.

demo.notificationBody: Only 4 more stamps until your free coffee. Keep it up!

channels.statement: Every loyalty update needs to reach your customer. Here's how each channel actually performs, and why the wallet is the right place for stamp confirmations, milestone nudges, and reward alerts.

steps.title: The customer gets stamped
steps.description: You scan the customer's QR code. Their stamp is instantly added to their wallet card.

steps.title: Our server detects the event
steps.description: Our system identifies the event type: new stamp, milestone reached, or reward unlocked.

steps.title: The notification is delivered
steps.description: Apple Wallet passes refresh via a silent APNs push; Google Wallet uses its Add Message API. Both platforms deliver directly to the customer's wallet — no third-party app required.

steps.title: The customer sees the notification
steps.description: The notification appears on the lock screen. The customer opens their updated wallet card with a single tap.

notificationTypes.title: Three moments, three messages
notificationTypes.subtitle: Each interaction triggers the right notification at the right time.

stamp.title: Stamp notification
stamp.description: Sent with every scan. The customer instantly knows their visit was recorded. Their card updates in real time.
stamp.notifBody: Only 4 more stamps until your free coffee. Keep it up!

milestone.title: Milestone notification
milestone.description: Sent when the customer approaches their reward. Creates anticipation and motivates return visits.
milestone.notifBody: Just one more stamp until your free coffee. We're waiting for you!

reward.title: Reward notification
reward.description: The moment of maximum satisfaction. The card clearly shows the reward is available.
reward.notifBody: Congratulations! Your free coffee is ready. Show your card to claim it.

advanced.title: Go further
advanced.subtitle: A few extras that make every automatic update more useful, plus the door to broadcasts when you want to talk to everyone at once.

features.title: Personalized messages
features.description: Show the customer's first name, remaining stamps, and reward name. Each notification reads like it was written for them.

features.title: Custom milestones
features.description: Trigger a message at the exact stamp count that matters. "Halfway there!" at stamp 5, "One more!" at stamp 9. 3 milestones per program on Growth, unlimited on Pro.

features.title: Campaigns & segmentation
features.description: Reach every customer at once with a broadcast, or target by stamp count, inactivity, and recent redemptions. Perfect for flash promos and product launches.

privacy.title: Privacy respected
privacy.subtitle: Your customers' data stays safe. We never compromise on privacy.
points: No personal data is shared with third parties.
Notifications go through Apple Push Notification Service — a secure, encrypted channel.
Customers can disable wallet notifications at any time from their settings.

sections.title: Stamp notification

problem.title: Your loyal customer forgets you. It's not their fault.
problem.description: Marc walks past your cafe every morning. But this morning, he's in a hurry. He doesn't think about his loyalty card with 8 out of 10 stamps. He goes to the Starbucks across the street, out of habit. The problem isn't competition. It's forgetting.

What you can do
with a broadcast
capabilities.subtitle: Everything you need to run real marketing campaigns, without an extra tool.

items.title: Segmentation
items.description: Reach only customers who matter for this campaign: new signups, inactive for 30 days, redeemed a reward last week, or collected at least 5 stamps.
items.badge: Basic on Growth · Advanced on Pro

items.title: Scheduling with timezone
items.description: Pick the local send time: Thursday 5pm, Saturday noon, or any moment that fits your business. Perfect for happy hours, lunch-break promos, or weekend openings.

items.title: Bilingual delivery
items.description: Write in English and French once. Each customer sees the language set on their device. No duplicate campaigns, no guesswork.

items.title: Delivery transparency
items.description: See exactly how many messages reached Apple Wallet, Google Wallet, got throttled by quota, or skipped because the wallet was uninstalled. Honest reporting, not inflated rates.

delivery.title: Delivery you can actually verify
delivery.subtitle: Every broadcast shows a per-provider breakdown, so you know where your message landed, not just that it was sent.
delivery.note: You see the exact number of customers reached, and we don't hide the cases we couldn't. Honest reporting, no inflated numbers.

useCases.title: Real campaigns, real businesses
useCases.subtitle: A few ways our partners actually use wallet broadcasts.

items.title: Café: Happy hour
items.description: "Every coffee €2 between 4pm and 6pm today." Sent to all cardholders at 3:30pm. A third walk in before closing.

items.title: Restaurant: Fill a slow night
items.description: "Tuesday is -20% on the tasting menu." Scheduled every Monday evening. Turns the deadest night of the week into the second-busiest.

items.title: Salon: Seasonal push
items.description: "Back-to-school blowout: €5 off any cut this week." Targeted to customers who haven't visited in 45 days.

items.title: Bakery: New product launch
items.description: "Our new sourdough is live today. The first 30 buyers get a free pastry." Broadcast to everyone with a loyalty card.

faq.title: Questions we hear a lot
faq.subtitle: Straightforward answers about wallet marketing for small businesses.

items.question: How much do wallet promotional campaigns cost?
items.answer: Nothing extra. Broadcasts are included in the Growth plan (3 per month) and the Pro plan (unlimited). There is no per-message fee, unlike SMS where each send costs €0.04 to €0.10 in France. A single SMS campaign to 500 customers can easily cost €30 to €50.

items.question: Do customers need to install an app?
items.answer: No. Customers install a standard Apple Wallet or Google Wallet pass, which is the loyalty card itself. Wallet notifications are a native feature of both platforms, already present on every modern iPhone and Android phone. No store download, no account, no permission prompts beyond adding the pass.

items.question: How does wallet marketing compare to SMS?
items.answer: Wallet notifications appear on the lock screen like an SMS, but they are free to send, reach around 85% open rate, and won't land in the iOS 26 "Unknown sender" filter. You also get built-in segmentation (by stamps earned, redemptions, or recent inactivity), which SMS platforms usually charge extra for.

items.question: Can I schedule campaigns in advance?
items.answer: Yes, on the Pro plan. Pick a local date and time, and the send goes out at the right moment in your business hours. The Growth plan sends campaigns immediately when you hit "Send", which fits most flash-promo use cases.

items.question: Can I target specific customers?
items.answer: Yes. On Growth you can filter by enrollment date (new signups vs longtime customers). On Pro you get advanced segmentation by stamp count, last redemption, and last-visit inactivity window, so each campaign reaches only the customers it's designed for.

items.question: Is this GDPR compliant?
items.answer: Yes. Consent is collected when the customer installs the pass, and they can disable wallet notifications at any time from their device settings. We never sell or share customer data with third parties, and customers can remove the pass from their wallet at any time.

items.question: Does it work with both Apple Wallet and Google Wallet?
items.answer: Yes. Every broadcast goes out to Apple Wallet and Google Wallet in parallel. Your customers see the notification whether they use an iPhone or an Android.

items.question: What happens if I reach the Growth monthly cap?
items.answer: You can either wait for the next month, or upgrade to Pro for unlimited broadcasts plus scheduling and advanced segmentation. Automatic notifications (stamp, milestone, reward) keep firing regardless, since the cap only applies to manual promotional campaigns.

privacy.title: Marketing that respects the customer
privacy.subtitle: Wallet broadcasts are designed to feel like a loyalty perk, not an ad.
points: Consent is collected at pass install. Customers can disable wallet notifications at any time.
The customer owns their data. They can remove the pass in one tap, and the notifications stop immediately.
One clean banner per broadcast, not a generic "Pass was changed" notice.
GDPR compliant. No advertising tracking, no data sold or shared.

timeline.title: 4 steps to become a founding partner

items.question: How many spots are available?
items.answer: We accept founding partners in waves of 10-15 businesses. The founding rate (€{growthFoundingPrice}/month for life) won't be offered after the program closes.

items.question: What happens after the 3 free months?
items.answer: You automatically switch to €{growthFoundingPrice}/month. We notify you 2 weeks before. If you don't want to continue, cancel in one click — no fees.

items.question: Do I keep the founding rate if you raise prices?
items.answer: Yes. €{growthFoundingPrice}/month for life means for life. Even if the Growth plan goes to €49.99 in 3 years, your rate doesn't change. It's contractual.

items.question: Can I change my mind and cancel?
items.answer: Anytime. No commitment period after the 3 free months. If you cancel, you lose access but your customers keep their cards.
